title: NBA Daily Rundown: July 5, 2026
subtitle: Your daily dose of hoops action and breaking news!

items.headline: LeBron James Eyeing Cavaliers Return?
items.summary: There's a 'growing belief' around the NBA that LeBron James may choose to finish his storied career with the Cleveland Cavaliers, according to reports. This sentiment suggests a potential return to Northeast Ohio for the superstar.

items.headline: Deni Avdija Deemed 'Untouchable' by Blazers in Trade Talks
items.summary: The Portland Trail Blazers have reportedly made Israeli forward Deni Avdija untouchable in trade discussions involving major stars like Giannis Antetokounmpo and Jaylen Brown. Avdija remains a central part of the team's long-term plans, highlighting the franchise's confidence in his all-around game and defensive versatility.

items.headline: Lakers Exploring Center Options, Knicks Sign Drummond
items.summary: After trading Deandre Ayton to Washington, the Lakers are actively searching for a new center, with Kevon Looney and Jonas Valanciunas surfacing as potential options. Meanwhile, the New York Knicks have signed Andre Drummond to a one-year veteran minimum contract after Mitchell Robinson departed for the Celtics.

items.headline: Jazz Edge Hawks in OT Thriller
items.summary: Rookie Darryn Peterson led the way for Utah in a close contest.

items.summary: Cameron Boozer impressed in his Summer League debut for Memphis.

items.summary: Darius Acuff Jr. powered Sacramento to a narrow victory.

items.summary: RJ Davis had a strong showing off the bench for San Antonio.

items.headline: Heat Duo Catches Fire
items.statContext: Jahmir Young and Ryan Conwell both put up 21 points for the Miami Heat.

items.headline: Rui Hachimura Not Expected Back with Lakers
items.summary: There's a growing expectation that Rui Hachimura will not return to the Los Angeles Lakers, potentially opening up new opportunities for the team.

items.headline: Jazz Ink Josh Okogie to Two-Year Deal
items.summary: Veteran guard Josh Okogie has reportedly agreed to a two-year, $12 million contract with the Utah Jazz.
